channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Tennessee nurse and his dog died trying to save a man from floods driven by Helene
item.description: “I’ve never had anyone risk their life for me,” David Boutin, the man Boone McCrary had set out to rescue said. “From what I hear that was the way he always been. He’s my guardian angel, that’s for sure.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Walmart will be closed on Thanksgiving Day for 5th consecutive year
item.description: Walmart will be closed on Thanksgiving Day for the fifth year in a row, the retail giant recently announced in its holiday plans.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, Fox 5

item.title: Judge rules Missouri sex offenders no longer need to post warning signs outside their homes on Halloween
item.description: A federal judge ruled that Missouri's sex offenders are no longer required to display "no candy" warning signs outside their homes on Halloween, arguing it is unconstitutional.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former New York governor and stepson assaulted during evening walk
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Former New York Gov. David Paterson and his stepson sustained minor injuries when they were assaulted on a Manhattan street by four men and a woman as they went for an evening walk, police said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Keanu Reeves spins out at Indianapolis Motor Speedway in pro auto racing debut
item.description: Hollywood star Keanu Reeves made his professional auto racing debut on Saturday in an event in which “The Matrix” star spun out at famed Indianapolis Motor Speedway.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Many loathe public speakerphone calls, so why do people do it?
item.description: Few things are as universally reviled as a loud phone conversation infringing on your personal space. (Mosquitoes and taxes, aside.) Doing it on loudspeaker is even more incendiary.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Child entrepreneur opens pizza shop with Caribbean twist in New York City
item.description: A young entrepreneur is bringing even more Caribbean flavor to New York City, opening a pizza shop with a Caribbean twist at 11 years old.
item.dc:creator: Zhané Caldwell, Fox 5

item.title: Where do Trump, Harris stand on marijuana policy?
item.description: In a poll taken last year, roughly 70% of American adults said marijuana should be legal — but where do the presidential candidates stand?
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New Jersey sisters battle stage 4 colon cancer together
item.description: They share a lot more than their sister bond. They are both in their late 30s and currently battling stage four colon cancer.
item.dc:creator: Grace Gomez, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: After the deluge, the lies: Misinformation and hoaxes about Helene cloud the recovery
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The facts emerging from Hurricane Helene's destruction are heartrending: Businesses and homes destroyed, whole communities nearly wiped out, hundreds of lives lost, hundreds of people missing.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Dallas Uber driver seen on video punching passenger after dispute over legroom
item.description: A 68-year-old Uber passenger was punched in the back of the head in Dallas, Texas, after he complained about the lack of legroom inside a car, a report says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rachel Morin's mom comes face-to-face with illegal immigrant murder suspect for first time in court
item.description: Rachel Morin's family came face-to-face for the first time with her suspected killer, an illegal immigrant who wore an "emotionless expression.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dolly Parton, Walmart announce millions in donations to Helene victims
item.description: East Tennessee's own Dolly Parton is expected to offer her assistance to help with the flood recovery efforts in Newport.
item.dc:creator: Hope McAlee, WGN 9

item.title: Ex- Virginia cop who killed shoplifting suspect acquitted of manslaughter, guilty on firearm charge
item.description: FAIRFAX, Va. (AP) — A jury on Friday acquitted a former police officer in Virginia of involuntary manslaughter after he fatally shot a shoplifting suspect outside a busy shopping mall.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BARAKAT and OLIVIA DIAZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: When will the power return? Weary Carolinas residents long for relief after Helene's fury
item.description: ASHEVILLE, N.C. (AP) — The weary and worn residents of Julianne Johnson's neighborhood in Asheville have been getting by without electricity since Hurricane Helene tore through the Southeast last week and upended their lives.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Los Angeles mayor picks ex-sheriff to be city's police chief
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Former LA County Sheriff Jim McDonnell has been selected to lead the Los Angeles Police Department, taking charge of the force of nearly 9,000 officers as discontent grows among the city's residents over public safety even as violent crime numbers drop, the mayor announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New judge could give Diddy another attempt to win bail
item.description: While Diddy's legal team hasn’t commented, the assignment of a new judge potentially opens the door for a third bail hearing.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Ohio woman's viral TikTok videos lead prompt search for body in her yard
item.description: Police in Columbus, Ohio have concluded an investigation after a woman posted on TikTok that she might have discovered a body buried in her yard.
item.dc:creator: Katie Millard, KRON4

item.title: Obama to campaign for Harris in key swing states, starting with Pennsylvania
item.description: Former President Obama is set to hit the campaign trail for Vice President Harris this month, starting his blitz in the key battleground state of Pennsylvania next week.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Need a passport? You can now get one faster
item.description: U.S. passport processing time has been reduced by two weeks at most, the State Department announced Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, Fox 5

item.title: South Carolina sets Nov. 1 execution as state ramps up use of death chamber
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — South Carolina's high court on Friday set a date of Nov. 1 to put to death a man who killed a store clerk a quarter-century ago, the second of an expected six executions in about six months as the state ramps up its use of capital punishment after a 13-year pause.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: For Pittsburgh Jews, attack anniversary adds to an already grim October
item.description: PITTSBURGH (AP) — Jewish communities everywhere reacted with horror at last year's Oct. 7 attack by Hamas on Israel, but the approaching one-year commemoration of the assault hits home particularly hard in Pittsburgh's Jewish community, which already marks a grim anniversary each October.
item.dc:creator: PETER SMITH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Boris Johnson: Netanyahu may have bugged my bathroom
item.description: Former United Kingdom Prime Minister Boris Johnson alleged in an interview with The Telegraph that his staff found a “listening device” in his personal bathroom after Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu had used it in 2017.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, Fox 5

item.title: Garth Brooks responds to sexual assault allegations
item.description: Country music superstar Garth Brooks has issued a statement following a lawsuit accusing him of raping a hair and makeup artist in 2019.
item.dc:creator: Brittney Baird, ABC 4

item.title: Twin babies who died alongside their mother are youngest-known Helene victims
item.description: A large tree had crashed through the roof, crushing Kobe Williams and causing her to fall on top of infant sons Khyzier and Khazmir. All three were found dead.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The Latest: Harris campaigns in Wisconsin and Trump in Michigan in battle for 'blue wall' states
item.description: Democratic presidential candidate Kamala Harris is opening a two-day stay in Wisconsin and Michigan, and Republican candidate Donald Trump will be in Michigan on Thursday as the two candidates grapple for wins in the “blue wall” battleground states, which also include Pennsylvania.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 3 officers convicted in Tyre Nichols fatal beating, 2 acquitted of civil rights charges
item.description: Three former Memphis officers were convicted Thursday of charges of witness tampering in the 2023 fatal beating of Tyre Nichols, and two were acquitted of federal civil rights violations in a death that sparked national protests and calls for broad changes in policing.
item.dc:creator: Megan Fayard, WGN 9

item.title: Twin babies who died alongside their mother in Georgia are youngest-known Hurricane Helene victims
item.description: Obie Williams could hear babies crying and branches battering the windows when he answered his daughter’s daily phone call last week as Hurricane Helene tore through her rural Georgia town.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NYC accelerates school leadership change as investigations swirl around mayor’s indictment
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City is speeding up its switch to a new schools chief as indicted Mayor Eric Adams faces mounting pressure to bring stability to a city government that has been roiled by searches, subpoenas and resignations.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Biden student loan forgiveness plan gets win in Georgia court
item.description: President Joe Biden’s student debt relief plan was dealt a win by a Georgia judge this week by removing one Republican challenger and letting the program advance for now after a temporarily restraining order expires.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Melania Trump says she supports abortion rights, putting her at odds with the GOP
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Melania Trump revealed her support for abortion rights Thursday ahead of the release of her upcoming memoir, exposing a stark contrast with her husband, Republican presidential nominee Donald Trump, on the crucial election issue.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINE FERNANDO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: VIDEO: Shipping container slams into electrical pole during Hurricane Helene; death toll reaches 200
item.description: A shipping container slammed into an electrical pole while floating debris was split in half during extreme, rising water in Asheville, North Carolina last week.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Woman recounts her rescue from river, husband's death
item.description: WASHINGTON COUNTY, Tenn. (WJHL) — Some of the best years of Vicki Hunter's life came to a tragic end Friday after a raging Nolichucky River ripped through the bottomland where she and her husband, Jerry, lived with more than a dozen horses.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Keeling, WFLA, NBC

item.title: VIDEO: Helene waves and storm surge crash into Florida home as owners watch
item.description: Terrifying moments caught on video showed Hurricane Helene’s powerful and deadly storm surge as it crashed into a home in Pinellas County last week.
item.dc:creator: Georgia McCarthur, WGN 9

item.title: Hurricane Helene scrambles politics in 3 battleground states
item.description: The 500-mile-long path of destruction cut by Hurricane Helene has scrambled the politics of three battleground states that could determine control of the White House and Senate: North Carolina, Georgia and Florida.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Shock of deadly floods is a reminder of Appalachia’s risk from violent storms in a warming climate
item.description: HENDERSONVILLE, N.C. (AP) — Hurricane Helene dumped trillions of gallons of water hundreds of miles inland, devastating communities nestled in mountains far from the threat of storm surge or sea level rise.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL PHILLIS and BRITTANY PETERSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Are LGBTQ Jews welcome in Orthodox communities? This is how they are building spaces of their own
item.description: It was a heart-wrenching choice. But when Daniel Gammerman decided to never set foot back in an Orthodox synagogue, he thought of it as an act of love. Not toward the Jewish community he was born into, but to himself.
item.dc:creator: MARÍA TERESA HERNÁNDEZ, Associated Press, ABC 4
